title: Staurogyne itatiaiae
taxonomy: Staurogyne itatiaiae belongs to the family Acanthaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the acanthus or acanthus family. This family is part of the larger order Lamiales, which comprises various flowering plants. The genus Staurogyne is a type of flowering plant known for its small, delicate flowers and slender stems. Staurogyne itatiaiae, specifically, is a species within this genus that is native to South America. It is classified as a type of shrub or herb, depending on the growth habit and environment in which it is found.
uses: Staurogyne itatiaiae, a species of aquatic plant, has been found to possess potential medicinal properties, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities.
